LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT SADDLEBACK HOMEOWNERS ASSOCIATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DENIED. DEFENDANT HAS FAILED TO SHIFT THE BURDEN OF PROOF IN THAT DEFENDANT HAS NOT DEMONSTRATED THAT PLAINTIFF CANNOT PROVE A CONTINUING NUISANCE OR THAT PLAINTIFF CONSENTED TO THE CONDITIONS; NOR HAS DEFENDANT ESTABLISHED UNDISPUTED FACTS NECESSARY TO ESTABLISH AN EASEMENT. THERE ARE TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT ON THE ISSUE OF A CONTINUING NUISANCE AS SET FORTH IN THE HERZOG DECLARATION. NO FOUNDATION HAS BEEN LAID AS TO THE ENABLING DOCUMENT, THEREBY NEGATING A MATERIAL FACT UPON WHICH DEFENDANT RELIES. THERE ARE TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT REGARDING WHEN ROCKS FELL SO AS TO PRECLUDE A FINDING OF AN EASEMENT. COUNSEL TO SUBMIT PROPOSED ORDERS. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar May-04-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT SADDLEBACK HOMEOWNERS ASSOCIATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT.
APPEARANCES: JOHN W. CHAPMAN, ESQUIRE & MARK G. INTRIERI, ESQUIRE (CHAPMAN & INTRIERI, 510.864.3600) APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF. H. GREGORY NELCH, ESQUIRE (LaMORE BRAZIER RIDDLE & GIAMPAOLI, 408.280.6800) APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT DeANZA. ABRAHAM MERTENS, ESQUIRE (650.364.8200) APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT DeANZA. THOMAS S. LAKRITZ, DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY (OFFICE OF THE CITY ATTORNEY, 415.554.6547) APPEARING FOR CROSS-DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO. THERE WERE NO OTHER APPEARANCES.
RULING: COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DENIED. DEFENDANT HAS FAILED TO SHIFT THE BURDEN OF PROOF IN THAT DEFENDANT HAS NOT DEMONSTRATED THAT PLAINTIFF CANNOT PROVE A CONTINUING NUISANCE OR THAT PLAINTIFF CONSENTED TO THE CONDITIONS; NOR HAS DEFENDANT ESTABLISHED UNDISPUTED FACTS NECESSARY TO ESTABLISH AN EASEMENT. THERE ARE TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT ON THE ISSUE OF A CONTINUING NUISANCE AS SET FORTH IN THE HERZOG DECLARATION. NO FOUNDATION HAS BEEN LAID AS TO THE ENABLING DOCUMENT, THEREBY NEGATING A MATERIAL FACT UPON WHICH DEFENDANT RELIES. THERE ARE TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT REGARING WHEN ROCKS FELL SO AS TO PRECLUDE A FINDING OF AN EASEMENT. EVIDENTIARY OBJECTIONS TO BE RULED UPON AT THE HEARING. COUNSEL TO SUBMIT PROPOSED ORDERS. (302/PJM/JF)
